title: Netflix Announces Sequel to Hit Film KPop Demon Hunters
summary: Netflix has announced a sequel to KPop Demon Hunters, its most-watched movie ever, following a new exclusive deal with co-directors Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans. The original film features Rumi, Mira, and Zoey from the K-pop group Huntr/x, who balance fame with their secret roles as demon hunters. It has received critical acclaim, with two Oscar nominations and a Grammy win for the song Golden, marking the first K-pop song to achieve this. In the announcement, Kang expressed pride in the Korean storytelling and excitement to expand the world. Singer Ejae, who voiced Rumi and co-wrote songs, hopes to incorporate diverse Korean music styles in the sequel, while producer Zhun aims to maintain the high energy and visual-music synergy. No release date is set, and the sequel builds on the seven-year development of the first film, reflecting its global success and cultural impact.

background_read: KPop Demon Hunters is an animated film on Netflix that blends K-pop music with fantasy action, following three members of the group Huntr/x who secretly hunt demons. Released after seven years of development, it became Netflix's most-watched movie, earning Oscar nominations for Best Animated Feature and Best Original Song, and a Grammy for its song Golden. The sequel announcement follows a new exclusive deal with directors Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans, highlighting the film's impact on promoting Korean culture globally. Creators aim to expand the story and incorporate diverse musical elements, building on the original's success.
Article_Structure: This article reports on Netflix's announcement of a sequel to KPop Demon Hunters, its most-watched film. Main points include the new exclusive deal with directors, the film's Oscar nominations and Grammy win, and creators' plans to showcase Korean culture. The purpose is to inform about the sequel's development and cultural significance. Evidence comes from direct quotes by Kang, Ejae, and Zhun, and factual data on awards, providing credible insights. Author credibility is implied through BBC sourcing, though specific expertise isn't detailed. Methodology involves reporting on official announcements and interviews, offering a balanced view of the sequel's prospects and creative goals.

perspectives.description: The creators emphasize sharing Korean stories and music, aiming to boost global appreciation.
